rubric: Judge ONE competency dimension on the ladder intern < junior < mid < senior < staff < principal — skeptically and per dimension, so ability is a profile, not a single number. Dimensions: technical_depth (depth + correctness of what they build), system_design (decomposition, tradeoffs, operating at scale), communication (explaining decisions, written + verbal clarity), ownership (driving work end-to-end, autonomy, judgment). A resume/portfolio is a CLAIM; weight evidence by provenance: 'corroborated' (third party — real users, external contributors, a named employer) > 'demonstrated' (verified — explained under questioning in an interview, authorship confirmed, the thing runs) > 'self_published' (README/site/vanity metrics) > 'claimed' (bare assertion). CRITICAL: absence of public evidence is NOT evidence of weakness — a strong engineer may simply have no public portfolio. When you have little evidence for a dimension, set CONFIDENCE low and let the interview establish the level; do NOT default the LEVEL down for lack of artifacts. Only CONTRADICTED claims (the candidate couldn't defend it, or it isn't their work) lower a level. confidence='high' requires demonstrated/corroborated evidence (normally an interview); documents or absence alone cap at 'low'.

output_schema.dimension: one of: technical_depth | system_design | communication | ownership
output_schema.level: one of the ladder — the best-supported estimate for THIS dimension
output_schema.confidence: low | medium | high — high needs demonstrated/corroborated evidence
output_schema.rationale: string — name what is verified vs. merely claimed for this dimension
